sound_type: raucous overlapping screams -- triggered flock alarm; winter beach or harbor flock suddenly disturbed; multiple birds simultaneously producing harsh sharp alarm calls; rapid cascade propagation through flock; kyow-kyow-kyow or keeow-keeow-keeow
ecosystem: Beach / harbor -- wintering flock; rocky shore, sandy beach, fishing harbor, breakwater, river mouth, tidal roost; urban and coastal environments; winter
season: Winter (primary PNW presence; largest aggregations December-March; alarm events most frequent at winter roost and foraging sites)
conservation_status: Least Concern (IUCN). Large North American breeding population. Some regional declines in eastern North America. Pacific Northwest wintering population stable. Common winter bird throughout PNW coast and Puget Sound.
milestone: Second Laridae entry at degree 290. Arc continues: colony ambient (289, E=6) transitions to triggered flock alarm (290, E=7). First winter-season entry in Laridae arc. Herring Gull is the archetypal gull of popular imagination -- the 'seagull' of cultural reference.

coordinates.description: Pacific Northwest winter range: coast and Puget Sound. Key wintering sites: fishing harbors (Westport, Ilwaco, Anacortes), river mouths (Columbia, Skagit, Snohomish), Puget Sound beaches and tidal flats. Year-round in small numbers; peak numbers December-March.

acoustic_data.vocalization_type: Triggered flock alarm -- not ambient chorus. Event begins when disturbance stimulus (raptor, human, dog) triggers alarm in birds nearest threat; propagates through flock in seconds.
acoustic_data.alarm_call: Loud harsh 'kyow-kyow-kyow' or 'keeow-keeow-keeow'. More emphatic and less melodic than contact mewing. Higher fundamental frequency than contact call. Optimized for rapid information propagation.
acoustic_data.frequency_range: 800-4,000 Hz overall. Alarm call higher frequency than contact call. Multiple simultaneous callers create broad-band acoustic energy within this range.
acoustic_data.flock_overlay: Dozens to hundreds of birds calling simultaneously at alarm peak. Total acoustic energy is function of flock size. 100-bird flock alarm approaches intensity of colonial chorus.
acoustic_data.event_duration: Typically 30 seconds to a few minutes. Brief and intense. Contrast with colony ambient which is continuous over hours.
acoustic_data.e7_calibration: E=7: triggered alarm event. Higher intensity per unit time than colony ambient (E=6) because it mobilizes maximum vocal output from all birds simultaneously. Shorter duration but higher peak intensity. One step below maximum flock intensity.

nodes.title: Triggered Flock Alarm -- New Acoustic Mode
nodes.description: Degree 290 introduces a different acoustic mode than 289: the triggered event vs. the ambient chorus. Colony chorus (289) is continuous, emergent, always present. Flock alarm (290) is sudden, reactive, event-driven. Both are social acoustic phenomena; different generating mechanisms. The acoustic modes of Laridae: ambient colonial, triggered alarm, colony overlap.

nodes.title: Laridae Arc Progress -- 289-292
nodes.description: Arc energy: E=6 (289, colony ambient) → E=7 (290, alarm event) → E=7 (291, colony) → E=7 (292, tern colony). 290 is the arc's energy pivot: from the single E=6 entry to the three E=7 entries. Colony → alarm → colony → tern colony. Summer → winter → summer → summer.

nodes.title: Winter Beach Flock -- PNW Ecology
nodes.description: Herring Gulls winter throughout PNW coast and Puget Sound. Key behaviors: fishing harbor foraging, salmon carcass exploitation at river mouths, tidal roost aggregations. Mixed-species flocks with Glaucous-winged, Mew, Ring-billed gulls. Salmon carcass foraging connects Herring Gull winter ecology to Pacific salmon runs.

nodes.title: The Archetypal Seagull -- Cultural Resonance
nodes.description: Larus argentatus is the bird most people visualize as 'seagull'. Large, white-headed, black-wingtipped gull of English seaside, American harbors, parking lot fast food. Named argentatus (silvery) by Pontoppidan 1763 for the gray back. Most-studied gull species in the world. Taxonomically complex: American Herring Gull smithsonianus distinct from European populations.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 + E=7 = E=15. Post-hardcore angular volatility paired with raucous flock alarm. Both triggered, both reactive, both produce simultaneous overlapping events. TAAS musical shifts vs. Herring Gull alarm cascade.
First Laridae E=6. Arc transition: colony ambient → triggered alarm. E rises 6→7. Summer colony → winter flock. Sustained ambient → brief intense event.
Third Laridae E=7. Arc returns to colony soundscape. Rocky island Pacific coast. Glaucous-winged x Western hybrid zone connects 289 and 291 biologically.
